description_long: This is a two-story apartment building with a steeply pitched, gabled roof covered in weathered shingles, showing signs of age and neglect. The exterior walls are primarily a faded off-white, with a horizontal band of darker, worn wood or siding running between the floors. Multiple windows, many of which are broken or missing glass, are symmetrically arranged on each side, and the frames appear dark and deteriorated. The main entrance is a single wooden door, partially open and located on the lower right of the front facade, with another door visible on the rear. The building's overall appearance suggests abandonment, with peeling paint, visible grime, and structural wear. The roof and walls show significant discoloration and damage, contributing to a distinctly derelict and historic look.
description: A weathered, two-story apartment building with a steep, shingled roof, faded walls, and broken windows. The structure appears abandoned and deteriorated, with visible damage and grime.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance door, five windows (three above, two below), and the most prominent weathering on the facade.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the gabled roof, three vertically aligned windows, and extensive wall discoloration.
description_view_2: Rear view with a secondary door, four windows, and more pronounced wall damage and fading.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a single door at the bottom, two windows above, and the gable end of the roof.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are symmetrically arranged in vertical and horizontal rows, with consistent spacing between them on each facade.
